completion_promises.criterion_text: Both wrappers in src/forge/tools/graphiti.py, each decorated with @tool(parse_docstring=True)
completion_promises.evidence: Created src/forge/tools/graphiti.py exporting guardkit_graphiti_add_context and guardkit_graphiti_query. Each is defined under @tool(parse_docstring=True). Verified at runtime: both are LangChain BaseTool instances, args_schema.model_fields includes doc_path/group/repo (and query/group/repo respectively), and the parsed description starts with 'Run `guardkit graphiti …`'. Tests TestDecoratorSurface.* pass (4/4).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Both call forge.adapters.guardkit.run() with a subcommand starting with 'graphiti ' so the resolver is not invoked
completion_promises.evidence: _invoke_graphiti() passes literal subcommand='graphiti add-context' / 'graphiti query' to guardkit_run(). The captured kwargs in TestSubcommandPrefixBypassesResolver.* assert subcommand == 'graphiti add-context' / 'graphiti query' AND .startswith('graphiti '). This satisfies run()'s _is_graphiti_subcommand() head-token detector (TASK-GCI-008) which keys off subcommand.partition(' ')[0] == 'graphiti', so the resolver-skip branch fires.

completion_promises.criterion_text: No --context flags appear in the assembled command line for either wrapper, even when a manifest exists in the target repo
completion_promises.evidence: _invoke_graphiti() does NOT accept or thread an extra_context_paths kwarg through to guardkit_run() (deliberately omitted vs the GCI-009 helper). TestNoContextFlagInArgv.* assert '--context' not in kwargs['args'] AND kwargs.get('extra_context_paths') in (None, []) for both tools. Combined with AC-002 (resolver bypass at run() level), no --context token can appear in the assembled argv.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Both return a JSON string; on internal exception, return {"status":"error","error":"..."} per ADR-ARCH-025 — never raises
completion_promises.evidence: Successful path returns result.model_dump_json() (canonical GuardKitResult shape with status/duration_secs/stderr/exit_code). Exception path goes through _error_json() which uses json.dumps to build {'status':'error','error':'<ExcType>: <msg>','tool':'<name>'}. TestReturnIsJsonString.* (3 tests) verify success/failed/timeout return parseable JSON with the correct status. TestInternalExceptionsAreAbsorbed.* (3 tests) verify RuntimeError/ValueError raised inside guardkit_run get folded into status='error' with the type name and message, including a quote/backslash payload that would corrupt naive f-string concatenation.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Function body wrapped in try/except Exception as exc:
completion_promises.evidence: Each @tool body is wrapped in try/except Exception as exc: returning _error_json(exc, tool_name). The shared _invoke_graphiti() helper has its own try/except Exception as exc: as defence-in-depth (so callers get the JSON envelope even if the helper itself raises). TestInternalExceptionsAreAbsorbed.test_*_returns_error_json_when_run_raises confirms exceptions never propagate out.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each wrapper logs via structlog with tool_name, duration_ms, status
completion_promises.evidence: Module-level logger built via _build_logger() prefers structlog.get_logger('forge.tools.graphiti') and falls back to a kwargs-accepting _StdlibStructLogger shim when structlog is absent (structlog is not declared in pyproject.toml). The success path calls logger.info('guardkit_graphiti_tool_invocation', tool_name=..., duration_ms=..., status=result.status); the exception path calls logger.exception(...) with status='error'. TestStructuredLogging.* (2 tests) patch logger.info to capture kwargs and assert the contractual triple is present.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests: each wrapper returns the right JSON shape on success/failed/timeout/exception; verify (via fake run()) that no --context flag is added regardless of the target repo's manifest
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/tools/test_graphiti.py has 21 tests organised by AC. Coverage: TestReturnIsJsonString (success/failed/timeout JSON shape), TestInternalExceptionsAreAbsorbed (exception → ADR-ARCH-025 envelope), TestNoContextFlagInArgv (no --context in args, no extra_context_paths), TestSubcommandPrefixBypassesResolver (subcommand starts with 'graphiti '), TestDecoratorSurface (parse_docstring schema), TestStructuredLogging (kwargs contract), TestModuleHygiene (no resolver import — uses AST walk to allow docstring mention but block actual imports), TestRepoConfinement (run() gets absolute repo_path under read_allowlist). All 21 tests pass; the wider tests/forge/adapters/guardkit/ + tests/forge/tools/ suite (242 tests) also pass — no regressions.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: No lint/format tooling is configured in pyproject.toml beyond pytest (no ruff/black/mypy section detected). The two new files follow the project's existing style conventions — type hints, from __future__ import annotations, 88-char target line length, docstring-first modules, snake_case functions — mirroring the sibling forge/tools/guardkit.py. Without a project-defined linter command to run, this AC cannot be deterministically verified in this turn. Recommend running whatever linter the CI uses against src/forge/tools/graphiti.py and tests/forge/tools/test_graphiti.py.
